collections.title: Story Plot
collections.subtitle: Ansh's Side
collections.description: Use the story index to uncover the mathematics behind each chapter, clue, and event.

items.title: The Calm Before the Chaos
items.summary: Meet Ansh before the nightmare begins and revisit the story moments that can later be connected to mathematical ideas.
items.concept: Rotational symmetry and repeating linear patterns govern the structural design of the haunted villa's central hall.
items.activity: Analyze the floor tile pattern and calculate the order of rotational symmetry before entering the next corridor.
items.history: Symmetry and tessellation studies date back to 300 BCE in Islamic geometry and ancient Indian mandala mathematics, formalized later by Kepler and Hermann Weyl.
items.graphDesc: Live interactive rotational transformation matrix showing tile reflection angles across axes.
items.videoSummary: Visual breakdown of how symmetrical spatial patterns create atmospheric tension in haunted villa design.

futureApps.title: Crystallography & Materials
futureApps.desc: Mapping molecular crystal lattices for next-generation superconductors.

futureApps.title: Computer Graphics
futureApps.desc: Procedural texture generation in ray-traced virtual environments.

futureApps.title: Robotic Navigation
futureApps.desc: Spatial mapping algorithms using SLAM and rotational invariance.

items.title: Enter at Your Own Risk
items.summary: Follow the forbidden entry point and collect the decisions, positions, patterns, and clues that shape the chapter.
items.concept: Vector displacement models Ansh's path through multi-level corridors with magnitude and directional heading.
items.activity: Plot the vector sum of Ansh's movement through the entry halls to find his net displacement.
items.history: Vector analysis was pioneered by William Rowan Hamilton in 1843 through quaternions and later refined by Josiah Willard Gibbs for physical mechanics.
items.graphDesc: Interactive 2D Cartesian plane rendering real-time vector addition and magnitude calculation.
items.videoSummary: Watch how displacement vectors determine optimal escape routes when visibility drops to zero.

futureApps.title: Autonomous Drones
futureApps.desc: Real-time 3D flight trajectory calculation and obstacle avoidance.

futureApps.title: Game Physics Engines
futureApps.desc: Simulating realistic collision dynamics and force fields.

futureApps.title: Satellite Trajectories
futureApps.desc: Orbital mechanics calculation for planetary exploration.

items.title: The Light Should Not Be Followed
items.meta: Chapter 3 · Light Intensity & Inverse Square Law
items.summary: Trace the chapter's movement, direction, and changing visual clues as material for later mathematical interpretation.
items.concept: Light intensity diminishes proportionally to the square of distance (I ∝ 1/r²), creating deceptive optic shadows.
items.activity: Calculate the light intensity at 3 meters versus 9 meters from the phantom lantern.
items.history: Formulated by Alhazen in 1021 and mathematically proven by Johannes Kepler in 1604, establishing modern optics.
items.graphDesc: Dynamic curve graph showing light decay as distance increases from the source.
items.videoSummary: Demonstrating how light attenuation deceives human distance perception in enclosed structures.

futureApps.title: Fiber Optics & Telecom
futureApps.desc: Signal attenuation management in transoceanic data cables.

futureApps.title: Astrophysics
futureApps.desc: Measuring star distance via standard candles and luminosity.

futureApps.title: LiDAR Sensing
futureApps.desc: Autonomous vehicle perception under low-visibility conditions.

items.title: When Beautiful Things Devour You
items.summary: Review the structures and transformations introduced in this chapter before their mathematical breakdown is published.
items.concept: Self-similar fractal geometry describes complex repeating organic and illusionary structures inside the villa.
items.activity: Compute the fractal dimension of a self-repeating branching pattern across 4 recursion steps.
items.history: Coined by Benoît Mandelbrot in 1975, revolutionizing geometry by describing non-Euclidean natural structures.
items.graphDesc: Interactive recursive tree visualizer adjusting depth, angle, and ratio dynamically.
items.videoSummary: An exploration of recursive self-similarity from Mandelbrot sets to haunted villa illusions.

futureApps.title: Antenna Design
futureApps.desc: Miniaturized wideband fractal antennas for 6G communication.

futureApps.title: Financial Modeling
futureApps.desc: Fractal market analysis predicting multi-scale volatility.

futureApps.title: Medical Imaging
futureApps.desc: Mapping bronchial trees and vascular networks in organ synthesis.

items.title: The Tongue of Stone
items.summary: Examine the stone language, symbols, and encoded relationships that can support future mathematical analysis.
items.concept: Modular arithmetic clock logic (a ≡ b mod n) decodes ancient inscribed stone runic ciphers.
items.activity: Decrypt a 5-letter rune sequence using a modular shift key of k ≡ 7 mod 26.
items.history: Developed by Carl Friedrich Gauss in Disquisitiones Arithmeticae (1801), founding modern number theory.
items.graphDesc: Interactive rotating modular wheel illustrating remainder congruence and encryption keys.
items.videoSummary: Step-by-step cryptographic decoding of ancient carved inscriptions using modular math.

futureApps.title: RSA Cryptography
futureApps.desc: Securing global internet transactions and digital signatures.

futureApps.title: Blockchain & Web3
futureApps.desc: Zero-knowledge proof verification and cryptographic hashing.

futureApps.title: Error-Correcting Codes
futureApps.desc: Reed-Solomon data restoration in deep space transmissions.

items.title: The Smallest Spark
items.summary: Complete the Part 1 index with the chapter's final signals, changes, and consequences ready for later study.
items.concept: Exponential functions (f(x) = a · e^{kx}) govern chain reactions and energy ignition cascades.
items.activity: Calculate the doubling time of energy spark propagation when the growth constant k = 0.4.
items.history: Discovered by Jacob Bernoulli in 1683 while studying compound interest, leading Euler to define e.
items.graphDesc: Real-time dynamic curve illustrating rapid exponential acceleration over time.
items.videoSummary: Visualizing how micro-sparks cascade exponentially into critical system reactions.

futureApps.title: Epidemiology
futureApps.desc: Predictive outbreak modeling and viral propagation control.

futureApps.title: Nuclear Physics
futureApps.desc: Subatomic chain reaction control in clean fusion energy reactors.

futureApps.title: AI Model Training
futureApps.desc: Gradient acceleration in deep neural network optimization.

collections.title: Enemy Records
collections.subtitle: Antagonist Side
collections.description: Inspect antagonist systems, tasks, traps, and technology through their underlying mathematics.

items.title: The Architect
items.summary: The Architect controls connected rooms and systems as a network of nodes and routes.
items.concept: Represent locations as vertices, connections as edges, and inspect paths, cycles, and network reachability.
items.activity: Sketch the control network and find a route that avoids a monitored node.
items.history: Leonhard Euler solved the Seven Bridges of Königsberg problem in 1736, giving birth to graph theory.
items.graphDesc: Interactive pulsing node network showing active connections, shortest paths, and offline nodes.
items.videoSummary: Tracing how antagonist node networks monitor villa rooms and how to exploit topology gaps.

futureApps.title: Internet Routing
futureApps.desc: BGP protocol routing optimization across global fiber backbones.

futureApps.title: Social Network AI
futureApps.desc: Community detection algorithms and influence propagation graphs.

futureApps.title: Power Grid Resiliency
futureApps.desc: Preventing cascading blackouts in smart electrical grids.

items.title: The Calculation
items.summary: A repeating antagonist calculation grows according to a detectable numerical pattern.
items.concept: Recognize arithmetic or geometric change, determine a general term, and estimate accumulated values with a series.
items.activity: Continue the system sequence and identify the stage at which its value crosses the warning limit.
items.history: Ancient Babylonian tablets recorded geometric sequences, formalized by Carl Friedrich Gauss at age 9.
items.graphDesc: Interactive step bar chart comparing linear growth versus exponential sequence accumulation.
items.videoSummary: Detecting hidden algebraic formulas inside automated adversary calculation loops.

futureApps.title: Algorithmic Trading
futureApps.desc: High-frequency time-series forecasting in global stock exchanges.

futureApps.title: Audio Signal Processing
futureApps.desc: Fourier series decomposition for noise cancellation headphones.

futureApps.title: Quantum Computing
futureApps.desc: Shor's algorithm for period finding in integer factorization.

items.title: The Trap
items.summary: The trap selects outcomes under uncertainty, but its possible results can still be measured.
items.concept: List the sample space, distinguish favourable outcomes, and calculate theoretical probability.
items.activity: Compare the available escape choices and select the path with the strongest probability of success.
items.history: Correspondence between Blaise Pascal and Pierre de Fermat in 1654 established mathematical probability.
items.graphDesc: Dynamic pie chart & decision tree calculating success probability across 3 escape paths.
items.videoSummary: Using expected value and Bayes' theorem to survive high-risk choices.

futureApps.title: Risk Assessment AI
futureApps.desc: Automated insurance and medical diagnosis risk prediction models.

futureApps.title: Game AI & Monte Carlo
futureApps.desc: Monte Carlo Tree Search powering AlphaGo and chess engines.

futureApps.title: Cyber Threat Detection
futureApps.desc: Anomaly detection screening network packets for malicious traffic.

items.title: The Purple Orb
items.summary: The orb models a system whose calculation requires both real and imaginary components.
items.concept: Write values in a + bi form and interpret addition, multiplication, and position on the complex plane.
items.activity: Combine the orb signals and map the resulting complex number to its plane location.
items.history: Introduced by Gerolamo Cardano in 1545 and geometrically unified on the Argand plane by Jean-Robert Argand in 1806.
items.graphDesc: Interactive complex plane illustrating real vs. imaginary vectors with phase rotation.
items.videoTitle: The Physics of the Purple Orb
items.videoSummary: Visualizing complex numbers in wave interference patterns and orb energy fields.

futureApps.title: Quantum Mechanics
futureApps.desc: Schrödinger wave function states governed by complex amplitudes.

futureApps.title: Electrical Engineering
futureApps.desc: AC circuit impedance and phasor vector analysis.

futureApps.title: Fluid Dynamics
futureApps.desc: Conformal mapping in aerodynamic aircraft wing design.

collections.title: Classwise Knowledge
collections.subtitle: Boards & Grades
collections.description: Preview classwise and gradewise mathematics before the complete curriculum library is released.

items.title: Class 6 Foundation
items.summary: A classwise preview of number systems, fractions, basic algebra, geometry, mensuration, and data handling.
items.concept: See how common Grade 6 foundations align across CBSE, ICSE, MP Board, and selected international pathways.
items.activity: Choose a foundation topic and compare the vocabulary and expected skill across boards.
items.history: Number systems originated in ancient Indus Valley (zero invention by Aryabhata) and Brahmagupta's rules.
items.graphDesc: Dynamic number line displaying positive, negative, and fractional value placement.
items.videoSummary: Core foundation breakdown spanning Indian state boards and international IB/IGCSE standards.

futureApps.title: Computer Data Types
futureApps.desc: Integer, floating point, and binary representation in software.

futureApps.title: Financial Literacy
futureApps.desc: Budgeting, percentage calculations, and interest fundamentals.

futureApps.title: Data Visualization
futureApps.desc: Infographics and statistical chart interpretation.

items.title: Class 7 Equations
items.summary: A classwise preview of rational numbers, equations, ratios, geometry, exponents, and data interpretation.
items.concept: Connect shared Grade 7 outcomes while keeping board-specific sequencing and depth visible.
items.activity: Select a topic and identify which prerequisite skill should be revised first.
items.history: Algebraic equations were systematically solved by Al-Khwarizmi in 820 CE in Al-Jabr, giving algebra its name.
items.graphDesc: Interactive algebraic balance scale balancing variable x against constant weights.
items.videoSummary: How to visualize both sides of an equation as a balanced physical scale.

futureApps.title: Chemical Balancing
futureApps.desc: Stoichiometry equation balancing in industrial chemistry.

futureApps.title: Economic Equilibrium
futureApps.desc: Supply and demand balance points in macroeconomics.

futureApps.title: Control Systems
futureApps.desc: PID feedback loops keeping aircraft and drones stable.

items.title: Class 8 Factorisation
items.summary: A classwise preview of linear equations, factorisation, graphs, mensuration, probability, and advanced number work.
items.concept: Compare Grade 8 topic progression across Indian boards and international grade-level expectations.
items.activity: Open a topic preview and map it to its earlier foundation and later application.
items.history: Factorization methods were developed by Pierre de Fermat and refined by Carl Friedrich Gauss for prime distribution.
items.graphDesc: Visual area rectangle decomposition of quadratic algebraic expressions.
items.videoSummary: Splitting complex algebraic expressions into intuitive geometric area tiles.

futureApps.title: Data Compression
futureApps.desc: Matrix factorization algorithms reducing image and video file sizes.

futureApps.title: Machine Learning
futureApps.desc: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) for dimensionality reduction.

futureApps.title: Cybersecurity
futureApps.desc: Prime factorization hardness underpinning digital encryption.

items.title: Class 9 Coordinate Geometry
items.summary: A classwise preview of polynomials, coordinate geometry, Euclidean geometry, statistics, and probability.
items.concept: Understand how Grade 9 concepts are grouped and assessed across boards without connecting this library to Ganith lore.
items.activity: Choose a chapter and inspect its concept map, prerequisite knowledge, and expected outcomes.
items.history: René Descartes created Cartesian geometry in 1637, bridging algebra and geometry for the first time.
items.graphDesc: Interactive 4-quadrant grid mapping points, lines, distance formula, and midpoints.
items.videoSummary: Navigating the 4 quadrants and applying distance and section formulas to spatial problems.

futureApps.title: GPS & Mapping
futureApps.desc: Global satellite positioning using latitude and longitude coordinates.

futureApps.title: Computer Vision
futureApps.desc: Bounding box coordinates for object detection in self-driving cars.

futureApps.title: 3D CAD & Architecture
futureApps.desc: Computer-aided blueprint modeling for structural engineering.

collections.title: Game Database
collections.subtitle: Rooms & Trials
collections.description: Use the game index to understand the mathematics behind rooms, rules, puzzles, and trials.

items.title: The Grid Chamber
items.summary: The chamber turns movement into positions, directions, and transformations on a grid.
items.concept: Apply coordinates, translation, reflection, and distance reasoning to navigate the room.
items.activity: Trace a valid grid route while preserving the transformation rule at every checkpoint.
items.history: Transformation geometry was systematically organized by Felix Klein in his 1872 Erlangen program.
items.graphDesc: Interactive grid matrix demonstrating translation, reflection, and rotation vectors.
items.videoSummary: How transformation geometry reveals safe tiles and traps in Mission 1.

futureApps.title: Game Development
futureApps.desc: Character skeletal animation and matrix transformations in Unreal Engine.

futureApps.title: Robotic Arms
futureApps.desc: Inverse kinematics controlling precision assembly line robots.

futureApps.title: Virtual Reality
futureApps.desc: Head tracking and spatial orientation in VR headsets.

items.title: The Angle Maze
items.summary: The maze responds to turns and intersections whose angle relationships reveal the correct route.
items.concept: Use complementary, supplementary, vertically opposite, and parallel-line angle relationships.
items.activity: Calculate the missing turn angles and reject paths that break the maze rule.
items.history: Euclid of Alexandria established axiomatic geometry in his treatise Elements around 300 BCE.
items.graphDesc: Dynamic ray-caster displaying incident vs. reflected angles at maze intersections.
items.videoSummary: Calculating turn angles to bypass automated laser gates inside Mission 2.

futureApps.title: Radar & Sonar
futureApps.desc: Beam steering and acoustic reflection processing in marine navigation.

futureApps.title: Optics & Photonics
futureApps.desc: Designing high-efficiency solar concentrators and laser lenses.

futureApps.title: Architectural Acoustics
futureApps.desc: Designing concert hall walls for perfect sound wave reflection.

items.title: The Equation Lock
items.summary: Each lock segment contributes a condition that must be satisfied by the same unknown value.
items.concept: Translate the lock rules into equations and verify a solution in every original condition.
items.activity: Solve the linked equations and test the result against all lock segments.
items.history: Systematic elimination method for simultaneous equations was recorded in Nine Chapters on the Mathematical Art (100 BCE).
items.graphDesc: Interactive graph finding the exact intersection point (x, y) of linear lock conditions.
items.videoSummary: Step-by-step graphical solution of simultaneous equations securing Mission 3.

futureApps.title: Circuit Analysis
futureApps.desc: Kirchhoff's current laws solving complex multi-loop electronic circuits.

futureApps.title: Network Flow AI
futureApps.desc: Linear programming optimizing logistics and global shipping routes.

futureApps.title: Structural Engineering
futureApps.desc: Truss stress analysis ensuring bridge and skyscraper stability.

items.title: The Probability Gate
items.summary: The gate changes among possible states, making strategy dependent on likelihood rather than certainty.
items.concept: Compare simple and conditional outcomes, then distinguish expected advantage from a guaranteed result.
items.activity: Calculate each gate option and choose the strategy with the best justified chance of opening.
items.history: Andrey Kolmogorov formalized probability theory using measure theory in 1933, creating modern stochastics.
items.graphDesc: Dynamic Markov chain generator calculating probability distributions across gate states.
items.videoSummary: Using conditional probability and expected value strategy to conquer Mission 4.

futureApps.title: Quantum Encryption
futureApps.desc: True random number generation from subatomic particle spin states.

futureApps.title: Weather Forecasting
futureApps.desc: Ensemble supercomputer probability modeling for hurricane paths.

futureApps.title: Automated Trading
futureApps.desc: Black-Scholes option pricing model managing investment portfolios.

quote: The more you know, the more doors you unlock. — Ganith
